title: 3 Free Taobao Keyword Research Tools
excerpt: Find hidden high-conversion keywords at zero cost
content:
Every Taobao seller knows that keywords are traffic entrances. How many people can find your product depends first on which keywords your title contains. But many beginners just copy others' titles or write a few words by gut feeling. The result: either competition is too fierce to rank, or search volume is too low for any traffic. Today I'm sharing three completely free Taobao keyword research tools — telling you how to use each one, what kind of keywords you can discover, and teaching you a golden formula for combining keywords. With these three tools, you can find genuine blue ocean keywords without spending a yuan — multiplying your organic search traffic.

First tool: Business Advisor's search term analysis. This is Taobao's official data tool for sellers, free to use once you have a store. Go to Business Advisor, click Market, select Search Term Analysis — you'll see search term data for all categories related to your store. Two core metrics: search popularity and online product count. Search popularity represents how many people search this term daily. Online product count represents how many products compete for this term. Divide search popularity by online product count — you get the theoretical competition level for that term. Higher values mean less competition and easier ranking. I suggest focusing on terms with search popularity between 1,000 and 5,000 and online product counts below 50,000. These are classic blue ocean terms — good traffic with low competition, ideal for small sellers entering blue ocean markets.

## Why This Tool Stands Out

For example, I helped a yoga wear student with keyword research. We found that the core term "yoga wear women" had 20,000+ search popularity but 300,000+ online products — too competitive for a new store, ranking on the first page was impossible. We continued scrolling through search term analysis and found "yoga wear plus size slimming" — search popularity of only 1,200 but online products under 10,000. Very low competition. After optimizing this term into the product title, within two weeks, the product's organic search traffic increased by 130%. And because competition was low, it quickly ranked on the first two pages, consistently bringing over 100 precise daily visitors. Business Advisor also shows each term's CTR and conversion rate. I suggest selecting terms with CTR above industry average — ensuring that when traffic comes in, people actually click to view.

The second tool is Taobao's search dropdown suggestions. Completely free, no tools needed — type a keyword into Taobao's search box and related suggestions automatically pop up. Most people just glance and scroll past, not realizing the tremendous value hidden here. Dropdown suggestions reflect real user search behavior — Taobao surfaces the most searched related terms here. For example, search "dress" and the dropdown shows "new dress," "slimming dress," "long sleeve dress," "summer dress," etc. These represent users' most concerned directions. Write down each term, then search each one again to see the next level of suggestions.

![](/images/ai-tools.jpg)

I call this the "deep dive method." Search "dress" and get a dozen related terms. Search each of those and get even more long-tail terms. For instance, search "new dress" might show "French new dress women" or "vintage new dress women." Keep digging in this manner, and in one session you can collect over a hundred long-tail keywords — all real user search terms. When I was working in the suit category, I used this method to discover "suit for office wear" — very low competition with decent search volume, consistently bringing 500+ monthly organic visitors. This method's advantage: data comes entirely from real users and updates in real time — more accurate than any paid tool. The downside: it's time-consuming. I recommend preparing an Excel spreadsheet and recording as you search — this prevents missing valuable terms and makes organization easy.

## Core Features Breakdown

The third tool is ChatGPT. Many people don't know ChatGPT can be used for keyword research — and with excellent results. The method is simple: give ChatGPT a clear prompt, like "As a Taobao e-commerce operations expert, help me expand 50 long-tail keywords for the following product: men's sports suit, stretch fabric, business casual. List them in four categories: core words, attribute words, scenario words, and selling point words." ChatGPT delivers a well-organized, fully categorized keyword list. Through repeated testing, about 60% of generated keywords are usable. The remaining 40% need verification through the first two tools — checking search popularity and competition levels — before filtering out the truly优质 ones.

ChatGPT has two major advantages. First, speed: manually researching 100 keywords might take two hours — ChatGPT does it in under 20 seconds. Second, coverage: human thinking has inertia, typically limited to familiar vocabulary. But ChatGPT isn't bound by thinking patterns — it can suggest cross-category keywords you'd never think of. For example, when I asked ChatGPT to expand yoga wear keywords, it gave me "yoga wear for office lunch break." At first, I thought this combination was odd — but when I checked Business Advisor, people were actually searching this term, and competition was very low. Such cross-scenario keywords are hard for human experience to generate but easy for AI to consider naturally. While humans are constrained by habitual thinking patterns, AI starts from users' life scenarios and uncovers search needs you'd never imagine.

Once you have keywords, how do you combine them into a title? Here's a formula proven through extensive testing: core category word + attribute word + scenario word + selling point word. Place the core category word at the very front — Taobao's search result display area is limited, with the first dozen characters being the most visible. Then add attribute words describing core product features — like "stretch fabric" or "cotton." Next come scenario words describing usage scenarios — like "business meeting" or "workout." Finally add selling point words — like "slimming," "wrinkle-resistant," "breathable." A title composed this way is both complete and logically structured. Taobao's system can understand your product attributes well, and users can immediately see key information.

## Step-by-Step Tutorial

A few technical details about keyword layout. The first 12 characters of the title are particularly important — this is what users see directly in search results without expanding. The first 12 characters must include the core category word plus one high-conversion attribute word — like "Men's Sports Suit Stretch Fabric" — so users immediately know what you're selling and its key feature, making them want to click. The latter part gradually adds scenario and selling point words, forming a complete title logic. Avoid spaces in the title — just connect words directly, as spaces waste precious character count. Don't repeat the same word — each core word should appear only once to maximize information density and coverage.

![](/images/tool-collage1.jpg)

Finally, keyword update frequency. Many sellers write a title and never touch it again, but market demand and search trends constantly change. For example, when summer comes, dress-related search terms add seasonal attributes like "sun protection," "thin," "breathable." In autumn, they shift to "long sleeve," "warm," "new autumn." So I recommend using these three tools monthly to re-research keywords, adjusting titles based on seasonal changes and trending platform searches. After each adjustment, observe data for a week — if traffic increases, keep the change; if it drops, revert. An experienced operator spending 2-3 hours monthly on keyword optimization can sustain organic search traffic growth. And with each optimization, you'll better understand what your target users search for. Over time, you'll develop keyword intuition — instantly spotting terms with potential.

In summary, each of the three free tools has unique applications. Business Advisor search term analysis is best for finding blue ocean keywords and checking competition data. Dropdown suggestions are ideal for mining real-user long-tail search terms. ChatGPT excels at batch keyword expansion, filling in blind spots from human thinking. Use all three tools together with the title composition formula shared above, and you can create high-quality titles without spending a yuan. The results are better than many paid SEO optimization services — because the data and strategy are in your hands. Open Business Advisor now, search your category, and see if there are blue ocean keywords you've been overlooking.

## Usage Tips

One more tip few people know: you can export search term analysis data to Excel, sort by search popularity in descending order, and filter for terms with high popularity but low online product counts. These are often blue ocean entrances overlooked by competitors. I do this filtering monthly and discover several new blue ocean terms to add to existing product titles, continuously bringing new organic search traffic to my store. The core of keyword optimization isn't achieving perfection in one go — it's continuous iteration, constantly discovering new opportunities. The longer you do Taobao, the more you realize that a well-written title can bring organic traffic for a long time — completely free with zero marginal cost.

Another practical detail: pay attention to keyword matching mode. Taobao's search matching mechanism combines partial matching and exact matching. Keywords in your title don't need to exactly match user searches — as long as core words are included, you'll be matched. For example, if a user searches "suit for office wear" and your title contains "suit office wear" or "office suit," you'll still be found. So when designing titles, consider natural word order variations — don't use just one expression pattern. Different permutations of the same meaning can cover more search scenarios.

Do weekly keyword data reviews. List the top five products in your store by organic search traffic, see which keywords they use, then apply these findings to other product title optimizations. The advantage: you don't need to research keywords from scratch for every product — directly copy verified effective keywords to other products. Highly efficient with great results. My own store tripled organic search traffic in three months using this method.
